latest.title: Disposal of Subsidiary: The GP Service Limited
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 11:30
RNS Number : 2251L
Kanabo Group PLC
11 December 2025
Kanabo Group plc (in Administration) ("Kanabo" or the "Company")
Disposal of Subsidiary: The GP Service Limited
11th December 2025
The Joint Administrators of Kanabo Group plc (the "Company") announce that on 9 December 2025, the disposal of the Company's entire issued share capital in The GP Service Limited ("GPS") was completed. The subsidiary has been acquired by Green UK Wholesale Ltd (the "Buyer").
The consideration for the transaction has been paid in full on completion. The specific terms remain confidential to protect the commercial interests of the operating business.
Following this disposal, the Joint Administrators are now focused on the Company's remaining structure. The Joint Administrators are currently working closely with the Company's Directors to evaluate strategic options for the future of the Company.
The objective of this review is to explore potential pathways that may allow the Company to exit administration as a going concern and to facilitate a potential lifting of the suspension of trading in the Company's shares.
Enquiries:
Administrator:
Damian Webb
damian.webb@rsmuk.com
T: +44 20 3201 8000

latest.title: Appointment of Joint Administrators
latest.content:
28 Nov 2025 16:02
RNS Number : 5085J
Kanabo Group PLC
28 November 2025
Kanabo Group Plc
("
Kanabo
" or "the Company")
Appointment of Joint Administrators
Kanabo Group plc announces that Damian Webb and Stephanie Sutton of RSM UK Restructuring Advisory LLP have been appointed as joint administrators of the Company with effect from today.
The Joint Administrators, working alongside the Board, will now assess a range of strategic options aimed at returning the Company to
financial stability
, with the intention of enabling the lifting of the current suspension and the subsequent restoration of trading in the Company's ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange.
Further updates will be provided as appropriate.

latest.title: Temporary suspension of listing
latest.content:
30 Apr 2025 14:47
RNS Number : 8832G
Kanabo Group PLC
30 April 2025
30 April 2025
Kanabo Group Plc
("
Kanabo
" or "the Company")
Temporary suspension of listing
Kanabo announces that given that the Company is currently unable to meet its ongoing obligations to issue its audited annual financial report and accounts ("Audited Report"), in line with its reporting deadline of 30 April 2025, its listing of its ordinary shares will be temporarily suspended with effect from market opening tomorrow at 7.30 a.m., Thursday, 1 May 2025.
When the Company completes its Audited Report, these will be announced to the market via a further RNS. Immediately after announcing the Audited Report, the Company will make application for the suspension of its listing to be lifted.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of Regulation 2014/596/EU which is part of domestic UK law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310).
For further information contact:
Kanabo Group Plc:
Avihu Tamir, CEO

latest.title: Business Update
latest.content:
4 Apr 2025 16:20
RNS Number : 7877D
Kanabo Group PLC
04 April 2025
Kanabo Group Plc
Business Update
4 April 2025
Kanabo Group plc ("Kanabo" or the "Company") (LSE: KNB), a leader in digital health services and specialised medicines (including medicinal cannabis) wishes to update the market regarding the Company's financial position and strategic direction.
On 4 March 2025, the Company announced that it continues to review its strategic direction and assess the need for additional resources to develop commercial AI technology. The Company's financial position has become more uncertain over the past month. The Company is engaged in ongoing discussions regarding a potential restructuring of its business, which may involve, among other options, asset disposals, mergers, or a refocus on the Company's core business lines. The Company is also continuing to assess its capital needs and funding requirements, including potential fundraising alternatives.
In light of the uncertainty regarding the Company's financial position, the Company anticipates that completion and publication of the Company's audited accounts for the year ended 31 December 2024 will be delayed.
Enquiries:
Kanabo Group plc
Avihu Tamir, Chief Executive Officer
Odelia Vazana , Chief Financial Officer
+44 (0)20 7469 0930
Peterhouse Capital Limited (Financial Adviser and Broker)
Guy Miller
/ Lucy Williams / Charles Goodfellow
+44 (0)20 7469 0930
About Kanabo Group plc (LSE: KNB)
Kanabo Group plc is a digital health company committed to improving patient care through its specialised technology platform and treatments, including medicinal cannabis.
Kanabo's NHS-approved telehealth platform, The GP Service, provides video consultations, online prescriptions, and comprehensive primary care. Treat It, the Company's specialised clinic, extends these services to chronic pain management and mental health indications, offering patients access to secondary care.
With two complementary business divisions, Kanabo delivers end-to-end digital health solutions, from telehealth consultations and prescriptions to tailored treatments. Its partially owned subsidiary, Kanabo Agritec Ltd, offers cultivation consultancy, enabling reliable access to high-quality raw materials for Kanabo's product range.
Kanabo Group plc remains focused on delivering high-quality medical solutions that make healthcare more accessible for patients across the UK and beyond.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Kanabo Group plc supplies industrial equipment and engineering services to large infrastructure and energy projects, handling everything from design and procurement to maintenance and support.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a sector that fuels major construction and energy initiatives. Its contracts with public and private developers give it a foothold in projects that are critical to national infrastructure and long‑term economic growth.
company_30s.current_state: Kanabo has been suspended from trading, has appointed joint administrators, and has sold off a subsidiary. The company is in the throes of a financial restructuring that could end in a fresh start or a formal exit from the market.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings paint a picture of a business in distress: a suspension of its listing, appointment of joint administrators, and the sale of a key subsidiary all point to a move toward restructuring.
Leadership changes – a director’s resignation – suggest that the existing management is stepping back while the administrators take charge.
Operational updates that highlight revenue growth and a business update are attempts to show that the company still has viable projects and that it can survive the turmoil.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent skepticism – the community has largely stayed quiet, with only a handful of posts expressing doubt or mild hope.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – there is no bullish chatter. The few hopeful voices are still far outweighed by caution.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the company’s suspension and administration signal a high chance of delisting or bankruptcy, and that any recovery is unlikely.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Early on the chatter was neutral, but as the suspension tightened and the administrators took over, the tone shifted sharply negative.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no recent search activity, which is a classic sign that a surprise catalyst could have a big impact.

near_term_catalysts: The first step is a formal resumption of trading once the administrators clear the regulatory hurdles.
A clear restructuring plan that outlines cost cuts, debt refinancing, or new equity injections will be the next trigger for a bounce.
Any announcement of a new partnership or a large contract win could signal that the business model still has value.
If the administrators manage to sell off the remaining assets at a price that covers liabilities, the company could emerge from administration.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a notice of trading resumption, a positive filing from the administrators, a spike in volume that breaks the compressed range, or a new funding announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early capitulation phase – the market has yet to find a new equilibrium. The APEX score shows a tight range, so a breakout that breaks the upper band would be the first sign of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Contrarian Bullish – the deep crash and silent market create a high‑reward scenario if the company can clear its administrative hurdles.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen more than 70% from its peak, which is a classic deep‑crash zone. If the company can get trading back on track and announce a viable restructuring, the price could rebound to halfway levels or beyond, offering upside of 10‑plus times the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The administration and suspension are major red flags. If the company cannot secure new funding or a restructuring plan, it may be delisted or go into liquidation, wiping out the share value entirely.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 10x or more. Potential downside: limited to the current trading level – the worst case is a total wipe‑out if the company is removed from the market.

playbook.if_bullish: Wait for the trading resumption notice. Enter with a small position that covers a few hundred shares, set a stop just above the recent upper band of the compressed range, and target a 2‑3x gain if the price moves to the mid‑range of its 52‑week spread.
playbook.if_watching: Monitor the administrators’ filings and any new contract announcements. If a positive restructuring plan is released or if the share price starts to climb above the upper band, consider a quick entry with a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is formally delisted or a bankruptcy filing appears, exit immediately – the risk of total loss is too high to keep the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Kanabo’s deep crash and silent market create a high‑reward chance that a clear restructuring will trigger a rebound, but a real risk of delisting keeps the downside real.
